title: Podman build adds labels if building the same image with different tags, unlike docker, buildah
body:
### Issue Description

I'm observing a discrepancy with regards to how `podman` build handles labels in comparison with the equivalent `docker` or `buildah` commands.

Building a `dockerfile` for a specific tag adding a specific label, and then building the same dockerfile for a different tag, without the label, has different results in `podman` , where the metadata is added to the new tag too. This is different from what `Docker` or `Buildah` do for the exact same command.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman build -t some-image:some-tag-with-labels --labels somefancylabel=true .
2. podman build -t some-image:some-tag-without-labels .
3. podman inspect some-image:some-tag-without-labels | grep 'somefancylabel'

step 3 should not have any match. 

Repeating the same steps but replacing `podman` with either `docker` or `buildah` does not produce any output (as expected?) on step 3.

I prepared a [dummy repo with a Github Action showcasing this behavior](https://github.com/Victoremepunto/test-image-labels/pull/1/files), feel free to check it, I hope is self-explanatory

### Describe the results you received

Step 3 - inspecting the image looking for labels - should not yield any labels

### Describe the results you expected

Step 3 yields labels when inspecting the second image and using `grep` to find matches for the label created for the first image

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Tested on Fedora 37, and on Github Actions with current `ubuntu-latest`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This is an issue in buildah, the thing is that it re-uses the intermediate image which has the label, you can try 
`podman build --no-cache -t some-image:some-tag-without-labels ` and it should work.

But this signifies that buildah's cache mechanism has a bug it uses intermediate image which has label associated to it.

This is happening because we are applying labels to all the intermediate layers as well instead of applying label only to the final image, one solution is to modify stage_executor so we only actually apply label to the final step and skip applying label to all the intermediate steps. @nalind WDYT ?

> podman build --no-cache -t some-image:some-tag-without-labels

That should work, but we loose the ability to reuse the cache which slows down our build time, unfortunately. 
Is there any other mitigation / workaround we could use ? we're working around this by either using `docker` in some pipelines, or adding the label to the Dockerfile at runtime (so we get a different hash) that won't be present on the release. 

It's a bit ugly but works for now, tough I would not like to keep it like this for much time

> This is happening because we are applying labels to all the intermediate layers as well instead of applying label only to the final image, one solution is to modify stage_executor so we only actually apply label to the final step and skip applying label to all the intermediate steps. @nalind WDYT ?

Yes, the bit in `StageExecutor.commit()` that applies the passed-in labels to every image should probably have been ditched in #4511, with the "append a synthetic LABEL line" logic from `buildDockerfilesOnce()` being appended to either the final stage or every stage instead.
